name: Irish Soda Bread/Maxie
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda. Set aside.
2. Cream the margarine and sugar in a large bowl.
3. Add beaten egg and buttermilk; blend well until smooth.
4. Gradually add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients. Mix with a wooden spoon only until the dough is moistened.
5. Fold in raisins, currants, and caraway seeds (if using).
6. Grease a 1-1/2 quart casserole dish and pour the batter into it.
7. Brush the top of the bread with melted margarine.
8. Sprinkle with a little sugar.
9. Bake at 375°F for 30 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 325°F and bake for another 30 minutes.
10. Test in the middle of the loaf before removing from the oven.
If buttermilk is not available, sour milk with vinegar can be used. The bread can also be wrapped and frozen for up to 6 months.
